content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

April 21, 2011

Anthony Lewis
General Counsel
California Technology Agency
1325 J Street, Suite 1600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-11-057

Dear Mr. Lewis:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

You are general counsel with the California Technology Agency. You are writing to request formal written advice on behalf of Michele Robinson, deputy chief (Data Processing Manager IV) of the Office of Information Security ("OIS"). Ms. Robinson has submitted a Form 700 which identifies investments (stock ownership) in several Fortune 500 companies that conduct business with the California Technology Agency. The companies include Microsoft, Cisco, Intel, AT&T, Alcatel Lucent, IBM, Verizon, and Hewlett-Packard. As deputy chief of OIS, Ms. Robinson can be required to provide technical assistance to agencies, review policies, develop security plans, and manage notification systems that are provided by or supported by the companies listed above.

You wish to know whether Ms. Robinson's investments in the above named companies would pose a conflict-of-interest with her duties as deputy chief of OIS. Please note that determining whether a conflict-of-interest exists in a given situation is necessarily a fact-sensitive analysis.

Because your inquiry is general in nature and does not involve specific governmental decisions, we have enclosed the following advice letters for your review: Reiter Advice Letter, I-06-113; Larson Advice Letter, I-06-073; and Reyes Advice Letter, A-04-210. We hope that

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. I-11-057
Page No. 2

these letters will be helpful in providing general guidance with regard to Ms. Robinson's
situation.

In the future, should you have questions regarding a specific governmental decision,
please contact us for further advice.

Sincerely,

John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

By: Emelyn Rodriguez
Counsel, Legal Division

ER:jgl

Enclosures

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Michele Robinson's stock investments in companies that conduct business with the California Technology Agency create a conflict-of-interest under the Political Reform Act given her duties as deputy chief of the Office of Information Security.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that the inquiry was too general in nature and declined to provide specific advice, instead directing the requester to three previously issued advice letters for guidance on similar situations and inviting contact for questions regarding specific governmental decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION/SHORT ANSWER sections. Contains explanation of the requestor's situation and general guidance approach. The FPPC provided referenced advice letters as enclosures rather than direct advice. Response letter from Assistant General Counsel John W. Wallace and Counsel Emelyn Rodriguez.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Michele Robinson's stock investments in companies that conduct business with the California Technology Agency create a conflict-of-interest under the Political Reform Act given her duties as deputy chief of the Office of Information Security.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC determined that the inquiry was too general in nature and declined to provide specific advice, instead directing the requester to three previously issued advice letters for guidance on similar situations and inviting contact for questions regarding specific governmental decisions.

embedding.summary: The FPPC responds to a request for advice regarding whether stock investments in Fortune 500 companies that do business with the California Technology Agency create a conflict-of-interest for an OIS deputy chief, but declines to provide specific guidance due to the general nature of the inquiry and refers to three prior advice letters.
